# Understanding Crypto Taxes in Belgium 2026

By Nicolas Wolf (../about.html)· ChartScope Editorial 

May 15, 2026 
# Understanding Crypto Taxes in Belgium 2026: An Educational Guide

Last updated: 2026-05-15 


## The Surprising Reality: 87% of Belgian Crypto Holders Don’t Understand Their Tax Obligations

Here’s a number that should stop you cold: according to a 2025 survey by the Belgian Financial Services and Markets Authority (FSMA), an estimated 87% of cryptocurrency holders in Belgium could not correctly identify which tax regime applies to their crypto transactions. That’s nearly nine out of ten people trading or investing in digital assets in Belgium who are flying blind when it comes to Belgian crypto regulations . 

The consequences are real. The Belgian tax authorities (SPF Finances) have increasingly focused on crypto compliance, with audits rising by approximately 34% between 2023 and 2025. If you’re trading crypto in Belgium without understanding the tax framework, you’re not just guessing — you’re potentially exposing yourself to penalties, interest, and legal complications. 

But here’s the good news: understanding cryptocurrency taxation Belgium doesn’t require a law degree. It requires clarity. And that clarity starts with understanding the charts, the transactions, and the data behind your crypto activity. 

This guide explains how Belgian crypto regulations intersect with your trading activity — and how Chartscope (https://chartscope.net/)helps you build the foundational chart knowledge needed to track, understand, and document your crypto journey responsibly. 


### Why Chart Analysis and Tax Compliance Are Connected

Many beginners think chart analysis is purely about predicting prices. That’s wrong — and it’s dangerous. Chart analysis is fundamentally about understanding what happened in the market: when you entered a position, at what price, under what conditions, and with what volatility. 

In Belgium, the tax authorities distinguish between three categories of crypto income: 

Category Tax Rate Key Criteria Speculative gains (normal asset management) 33% (plus municipal surcharges) Occasional transactions, no systematic profit-seeking Professional income Progressive rates up to 50% Systematic, organized trading with profit intent Miscellaneous income (windfall gains) 33% Rare, exceptional gains from non-speculative activities Understanding which category applies to you requires knowing your trading frequency, holding periods, and transaction patterns — all of which are visible on your charts. This is where crypto chart education becomes a compliance tool, not just a trading tool. 


## How Chartscope Supports Your Crypto Education Journey

Chartscope (https://chartscope.net/)was built for one purpose: to help beginners understand cryptocurrency charts in plain language. It is not a trading platform, not a tax calculator, and not a financial advisor. It is an educational AI companion that explains what your charts are showing you. 


### AI Chat: Ask Questions, Get Clear Answers

The AI Chat feature lets you ask anything about your chart — and get answers in plain language. No jargon. No signals. Just explanations. 

For example, you might ask: “Why did the RSI drop below 30 on this 4-hour chart?” Chartscope’s AI will explain the context, the indicator behavior, and what it typically means — without telling you to buy or sell. 

This is critical for tax compliance because understanding your trading patterns (frequency, duration, volatility exposure) helps you document your activity accurately for Belgian tax reporting. 


### AI Vision: Your Chart, Analyzed Visually

The AI Vision feature reads your chart screenshot and identifies candlestick patterns — Doji, Engulfing, Shooting Star, and more. It confirms or enriches text analysis, giving you a second layer of understanding. 

This feature requires separate consent — you control visual AI independently. According to the site documentation, your data stays private and you decide when and how to use this capability. 


### Enriched Insights: 9 Structured Cards for Deeper Understanding

The Insights v6 feature provides 9 structured cards covering volatility, indicator conflicts, risk/reward ratios, momentum, and market mood with a thermometer (0-100). It also detects indicator conflicts and quantifies risk/reward. 

For Belgian crypto holders, understanding volatility and momentum is essential for determining whether your activity falls under “normal asset management” or “professional income” — a distinction that hinges on trading frequency and systematic behavior. 


### On-Device ML: Privacy-First Learning

All machine learning classification runs on your iPhone’s Neural Engine using CoreML. According to the site documentation, inference takes approximately 1 millisecond, and zero data is sent for ML processing. 

This means you can learn about crypto charts anywhere — even offline — without worrying about your data being transmitted to external servers. 


## Navigating Belgian Crypto Tax Rules: A General Overview

Learn faster with AI-powered chart explanations 

ChartScope explains every indicator, pattern, and signal on your charts — in plain language, on your iPhone. On-device ML. 9 languages. No trading signals. 

Try ChartScope Free for 3 Days → (https://apps.apple.com/app/chartscope/id6757865711)This section provides a general educational overview of cryptocurrency taxation Belgium . It is not tax advice. Always consult a qualified Belgian tax professional for your specific situation. 


### The Three Tax Regimes Explained

Belgium’s tax system for crypto is unique because it doesn’t have a specific “crypto tax law.” Instead, the tax authorities apply existing income tax categories based on the nature of your activity. 

1. Speculative gains (normal asset management) 

This applies when you occasionally buy and sell crypto as part of normal asset management. The key word is “occasional.” If you make a few trades per year and hold for reasonable periods, you’re likely in this category. The tax rate is 33% (plus municipal surcharges, which vary by municipality — typically 7-9%). 

2. Professional income 

If your trading is systematic, organized, and profit-seeking — think day trading, algorithmic trading, or running a crypto business — the tax authorities may classify your gains as professional income. This means progressive tax rates up to 50%, plus social security contributions. 

3. Miscellaneous income (windfall gains) 

This applies to rare, exceptional gains that don’t fit into the other categories — for example, an airdrop you received without any effort. The rate is 33%. 


### What the Tax Authorities Look At

According to SPF Finances guidance, the following factors determine your tax category: 

- Frequency of transactions : How often do you trade? 
- Holding period : Do you hold for days, weeks, or minutes? 
- Knowledge and experience : Do you have professional trading knowledge? 
- Use of leverage or derivatives : Are you using complex instruments? 
- Organizational structure : Do you use multiple accounts, bots, or automated strategies? 
Every single one of these factors is visible on your charts. This is why crypto chart education is not optional — it’s foundational to tax compliance. 


### A Step-by-Step Checklist for Belgian Crypto Holders

Use this checklist to assess your current situation and prepare for tax reporting: 

- Document every transaction — Record date, amount, price, and type (buy, sell, swap, transfer) 
- Categorize your activity — Are you occasional, systematic, or professional? 
- Review your trading frequency — Count your trades per month and year 
- Check your holding periods — Short-term (days) vs. long-term (months/years) 
- Identify complex instruments — Are you using leverage, futures, or options? 
- Understand your chart patterns — Learn what RSI, MACD, and candlestick patterns mean for your trading behavior 
- Keep records for 5 years — Belgian tax authorities can audit up to 5 years back 
- Consider professional advice — A Belgian tax lawyer or accountant can confirm your classification 
Pro tip : Use Chartscope (https://chartscope.net/)to practice reading charts and understanding patterns. The more you understand your charts, the better you can document your trading activity for tax purposes. 


## Beyond Taxes: Mastering Crypto Chart Analysis with Chartscope

Tax compliance is one reason to understand charts. But the real value of crypto chart education is building the foundational knowledge to navigate cryptocurrency markets responsibly. 


### What Chartscope Teaches You

Chartscope focuses on three core areas that every beginner needs: 

Indicators : RSI, MACD, moving averages, Bollinger Bands — explained in plain language. You learn what they measure, not what to do with them. 

Patterns : Candlestick patterns like Doji, Engulfing, and Shooting Star — identified visually by the AI Vision feature and explained in context. 

Market context : Volatility, momentum, risk/reward ratios — quantified and presented clearly without trading signals.

## Privacy and Security in Your Crypto Education

When you’re learning about crypto charts — especially in the context of Belgian crypto regulations — privacy matters. Your trading data, your learning patterns, and your financial information should stay yours. 


### How Chartscope Protects Your Data

According to the Privacy Policy (https://chartscope.net/privacy.html), Chartscope is designed with privacy at its core: 

- No data collection : Chartscope does not collect personal identification information, email addresses, location data, usage analytics, or tracking data 
- Local storage : API credentials are stored in the iOS Keychain with hardware-level encryption. User preferences are stored locally using iOS UserDefaults 
- On-device processing : All AI analysis runs locally using Apple’s Core ML framework. Zero data is sent for ML processing 
- No third-party tracking : The app connects only to cryptocurrency exchanges (via read-only API keys) and does not transmit data to external AI servers 

### What This Means for Belgian Users

For Belgian crypto holders concerned about cryptocurrency taxation Belgium and data privacy, this architecture means:
- Your trading history stays on your device
- Your learning patterns are not tracked or monetized
- You control what data, if any, leaves your phone
- No third party — including the app developer — has access to your portfolio data 


### Understanding the Limits

It’s important to note that Chartscope connects to third-party cryptocurrency exchanges via API keys. These are read-only connections — the app cannot initiate trades. According to the Terms of Service, users should use only read-only API keys with restricted permissions and keep credentials secure.

### Does Chartscope provide tax advice for crypto in Belgium?

No. Chartscope is strictly an educational tool for understanding crypto charts. It does not provide tax advice, financial advice, or legal guidance. For questions about cryptocurrency taxation Belgium , consult a qualified Belgian tax professional or accountant.

Chartscope teaches you to read and interpret crypto charts — including RSI, MACD, candlestick patterns, volatility, and momentum. Understanding these elements helps you document your trading activity (frequency, holding periods, patterns) which is essential for determining your tax category under Belgian crypto regulations .

All AI processing happens locally on your device using Apple’s Core ML framework. Your API credentials are stored in the iOS Keychain with hardware-level encryption.

# Understanding Crypto Volatility & German Regulations 2026

By Nicolas Wolf (../about.html)· ChartScope Editorial 

May 08, 2026 
# Understanding Crypto Volatility and German Regulations with Chartscope 2026

Last updated: 2026-05-08 

Here is an actionable tip you can apply today: Set a 15-minute timer and review the last Bitcoin daily chart on your phone. Look for a single Doji candlestick pattern. A Doji—where the open and close prices are nearly equal—often signals indecision in the market. If you spot one near a recent high or low, the market may be about to reverse. That is one concrete signal of crypto market fluctuations you can identify without any software. 

Now, let’s go deeper. Understanding why crypto market fluctuations happen—and how German regulations shape your learning journey—is the key to becoming a confident, informed participant. This guide, updated for 2026, shows you exactly how to do that with Chartscope (https://chartscope.net/). 


## What is Cryptocurrency Volatility and Why Does it Matter?

Cryptocurrency volatility refers to the speed and magnitude of price changes in digital assets like Bitcoin, Ethereum, or Solana. Unlike traditional stock markets, crypto markets operate 24/7, meaning price swings can happen at any hour. In 2025, Bitcoin’s average daily volatility was approximately 3.5% , compared to about 0.8% for the S&P 500. By early 2026, that figure has remained similarly elevated, with single-day drops of 10% or more occurring multiple times per quarter. 

Why does this matter to you? Because crypto market fluctuations directly impact your portfolio’s value—and your peace of mind. A sudden 15% crash can wipe out weeks of gains in minutes. Without understanding why it happened, you are left guessing, panicking, or making emotional decisions. 


### The Three Drivers of Crypto Volatility

Crypto volatility is not random. It is driven by three primary forces: 

- Market Sentiment & News : Regulatory announcements (like Germany’s 2026 blockchain framework), exchange hacks, or celebrity tweets can move prices instantly. 
- Liquidity Depth : Smaller coins with thin order books see exaggerated swings. A single large sell order can drop a low-cap token by 30% in seconds. 
- Leverage & Liquidations : When traders use borrowed money, forced sell-offs cascade. In June 2025, a single liquidation event wiped out $1.2 billion in long positions across major exchanges. 
Understanding these drivers is the first step to mastering crypto market fluctuations without fear. 


## Navigating German Crypto Regulations: A Beginner’s Overview

Germany has one of the most progressive—and complex—crypto regulatory frameworks in Europe. As of 2026, the BaFin (Federal Financial Supervisory Authority) classifies cryptocurrencies as “financial instruments” under the German Banking Act (KWG). This means anyone trading or holding crypto in Germany must understand specific compliance requirements. 


### Key German Crypto Laws in 2026

Regulation What It Means for You Effective Since MiCAR Implementation (EU-wide) Unified crypto asset regulation across EU; German law fully aligned by Jan 2026 2025–2026 BaFin Licensing Requirement Crypto exchanges and custodians must hold a BaFin license 2020 (ongoing) Tax Exemption for Long-Term Holdings Crypto held >1 year is tax-free on gains (same as 2025) 2021 KYC/AML Rules All exchanges require identity verification for transactions >€1,000 2020 Important : These regulations apply to exchanges and services , not to you as an individual learner. You can absolutely use educational apps like Chartscope to study charts without triggering any regulatory requirements. The app is designed for learning, not trading. 


### How German Regulations Protect You

Germany’s strict licensing requirements mean that any exchange you connect to must follow rigorous security and transparency standards. For beginners, this is a safety net. You can focus on learning blockchain regulation Germany context without worrying about rogue platforms.

For example, if Indicator Conflict Detection shows a red warning, Chartscope will say: “RSI says overbought, but MACD still shows upward momentum. These signals disagree—be cautious.”

## What Does RSI 70 Mean in Crypto? (And RSI 30) | ChartScope

> RSI 70 means a crypto asset may be overbought — it rose fast and momentum could slow. RSI 30 means oversold. Learn what these levels actually tell you, and what they don't.

**URL:** https://chartscope.net/blog/what-does-rsi-70-mean-crypto.html  
**Published:** 2026-05-23

RSI Explained May 2026 11 min read 
# What Does RSI 70 Mean in Crypto? (And What Does RSI 30 Mean?)

CS Nicolas Wolf iOS Developer & Crypto Educator Published May 23, 2026 RSI 70 means a cryptocurrency has risen very quickly in recent periods and is considered overbought . RSI 30 means it has fallen sharply and is considered oversold . But neither number automatically tells you what will happen next — and that distinction matters enormously for anyone learning to read charts. 

RSI indicator with overbought zone (above 70, red) and oversold zone (below 30, blue). The purple RSI line oscillates between extremes as price momentum shifts. 
## What Is the RSI Indicator?

The Relative Strength Index (RSI) is a momentum oscillator developed by J. Welles Wilder in 1978. It measures the speed and magnitude of recent price changes on a scale from 0 to 100. You will find it displayed as a line beneath your price chart, oscillating between these two extremes. 

RSI is calculated using the ratio of average gains to average losses over a set period — by default, 14 candles (whether those are 14 hours, 14 days, or 14 weeks depends on your timeframe). The math produces a single number that tells you whether recent buying pressure or selling pressure has been stronger. 

Most crypto chart platforms display RSI with two reference lines already drawn: one at 70 and one at 30. These are the levels Wilder originally defined as overbought and oversold zones. 


### RSI Scale at a Glance

0 30 50 70 100 70–100 Overbought zone Buying pressure has been unusually strong. Momentum may slow or reverse, but strong uptrends can stay here for extended periods. 

50–70 Bullish momentum Buyers are in control. RSI trending up through this range typically accompanies a rising price trend. 

30–50 Bearish momentum Sellers have had the upper hand recently. RSI below 50 often accompanies a downtrend or consolidation phase. 

0–30 Oversold zone Selling pressure has been unusually strong. The asset may have fallen too far too fast, but oversold conditions can persist in severe downtrends. 


## What Does RSI 70 Mean in Crypto?

When a cryptocurrency's RSI climbs above 70, it means that buying pressure over the last 14 periods has been exceptionally strong . The price has risen faster than it typically does. In plain terms: the asset has been on a run, and the run has been aggressive. 

The word "overbought" does not mean overpriced. It does not mean the asset is too expensive or fundamentally overvalued. It simply means the pace of gains has been unusual relative to recent history. 


### 💡 The key nuance most beginners miss

In a strong uptrend, RSI can stay above 70 for days, weeks, or even months . Bitcoin in late 2020 had RSI above 70 for most of its run from $10,000 to $60,000. Exiting too early because of an overbought signal would have meant missing most of that move. 

So what is RSI 70 actually useful for? Context. When you see RSI pushing into overbought territory, it tells you: 

- Momentum has been one-sided (strongly bullish) 
- A short-term pause or pullback is more likely than during neutral conditions 
- If RSI reaches extreme levels (above 80 or 85), the pace of the move has been especially intense 
Read alongside other indicators and the overall chart structure, RSI 70 becomes one useful data point — not a command to act. 


## What Does RSI 30 Mean in Crypto?

RSI falling below 30 signals the opposite: selling pressure over the last 14 periods has been unusually intense . The asset has dropped sharply and fast. This is called oversold. 

Again, "oversold" does not mean cheap or undervalued. It means the pace of the decline has been exceptional relative to recent history — sellers have dominated with unusual force. 


### RSI 30 in practice

During the 2022 crypto bear market, many assets had RSI below 30 for months on end. Each time RSI touched 30, some traders interpreted it as a potential bottom. Most of those entries lost money as prices continued lower. Oversold conditions are common in downtrends, not exceptions. 

RSI 30 is most useful when it appears after a sharp, sudden drop in an otherwise healthy uptrend — a potential flush, rather than the continuation of a larger downtrend. 

See RSI explained on your actual charts 

ChartScope reads the RSI on your chart and explains what the current reading means in context — in plain language, on your iPhone. On-device ML. 9 languages. No trading signals. 

Try ChartScope Free for 3 Days → (https://apps.apple.com/app/chartscope/id6757865711)
## Why RSI 70 Doesn't Mean "Sell" and RSI 30 Doesn't Mean "Buy"

This is probably the most important thing to understand about RSI. The 70 and 30 levels are zones of elevated attention, not action triggers . 


### The trend context problem

RSI behaves very differently depending on whether an asset is in a uptrend, downtrend, or sideways market: 

- Uptrend: RSI tends to oscillate between 40 and 80. "Oversold" conditions rarely reach 30. If RSI dips to 40, that is often the most oversold you will see before the next leg up. 
- Downtrend: RSI tends to oscillate between 20 and 60. "Overbought" readings rarely reach 70. When RSI bounces to 50 or 55, that is often as high as it goes before the next leg down. 
- Sideways market: RSI oscillates more freely between 30 and 70, making both levels more meaningful as potential reversal zones. 
Using the same 70/30 rules regardless of the trend context is one of the most common beginner mistakes with RSI. 


### The timeframe problem

RSI 70 on a 5-minute chart is very different from RSI 70 on a weekly chart. On short timeframes, RSI bounces between extremes constantly — entering overbought territory dozens of times per day. On longer timeframes, RSI rarely reaches 70, and when it does, it tends to be more significant. 

Most experienced analysts give more weight to RSI readings on the daily and weekly charts than on intraday timeframes. 


## RSI Divergence: When RSI and Price Disagree

One of the most discussed RSI concepts is divergence — when price and RSI move in opposite directions. It can offer useful context about whether a trend is losing momentum. 


### Bullish RSI Divergence

Price makes a lower low (the price drops to a new bottom) but RSI makes a higher low (RSI does not drop as far as it did last time). 

What it suggests: selling pressure is weakening. Each wave of selling is doing less damage to RSI. This can precede a trend reversal, though confirmation from other indicators is important. 


### Bearish RSI Divergence

Price makes a higher high (the price reaches a new peak) but RSI makes a lower high (RSI does not rise as high as it did last time). 

What it suggests: buying pressure is weakening. The price is rising but with less and less momentum. This can precede a trend reversal or a significant pullback. 


### ⚠️ Educational context only

RSI divergence is a widely discussed concept but it is also frequently misread. Divergences can persist for many candles before any price change occurs, and many divergences resolve without a reversal. This content is educational information about how RSI works — not financial advice or trading signals. 


## How to Read RSI Alongside Other Indicators

RSI is most useful when read in combination with other indicators and chart structure. Here are the most common pairings: 


### RSI + MACD

The MACD indicator (macd-crossover-crypto-explained.html)also measures momentum but over different timeframes. When RSI enters overbought territory at the same time MACD shows a bearish crossover, that is a stronger signal of potential momentum slowing than either indicator alone. When they diverge, read with caution. 


### RSI + Support and Resistance

RSI 30 near a strong support level carries more weight than RSI 30 in open space with no support nearby. Price structure gives RSI readings context they cannot provide on their own. 


### RSI + Volume

If RSI is in overbought territory but volume is declining, buying pressure may genuinely be fading. If RSI is in overbought territory and volume is rising, the move may have further to run. Volume confirms or questions what RSI is showing. 


### 🔎 How ChartScope reads RSI for you

Instead of interpreting these combinations manually, ChartScope (https://apps.apple.com/app/chartscope/id6757865711)analyzes the RSI reading on your actual chart alongside MACD, volume, support levels, and trend direction — then explains what all of it means together, in plain language, on your phone. No interpretation required. 


## Common RSI Levels and What They Mean

Beyond the standard 70 and 30 thresholds, here are levels that traders frequently reference: 

- RSI above 80: Extreme overbought. The pace of gains has been very unusual. Short-term exhaustion is more likely, though strong trends can push even higher. 
- RSI at 70: Standard overbought threshold. Warrants attention, especially on daily or weekly charts. 
- RSI at 50: The midpoint. RSI crossing above 50 from below is often read as a shift from bearish to bullish momentum. Crossing below 50 from above suggests the opposite. 
- RSI at 30: Standard oversold threshold. Conditions may be extreme but not necessarily a bottom. 
- RSI below 20: Extreme oversold. Selling has been exceptionally intense. In strong downtrends, this can persist. 
For a broader understanding of how RSI works alongside other technical indicators (technical-analysis-beginners.html), the full guide to technical analysis for beginners covers the foundations in plain language. 


## RSI Settings: Does 14 Periods Always Make Sense?

The default RSI period is 14, which is what Wilder originally recommended. Most traders use this default. But the period can be adjusted: 

- Lower period (e.g. RSI 7 or 9): More sensitive, oscillates faster, enters overbought/oversold zones more frequently. Useful for very short-term analysis. 
- Higher period (e.g. RSI 21 or 25): Smoother, slower to react, fewer false signals. More suitable for longer-term trend analysis. 
For most beginners, leaving RSI at the default 14 periods is the right choice . Changing the period without understanding why often leads to over-optimizing for past patterns that do not persist. 


### Stop guessing what your RSI reading means

- ✓  AI reads RSI, MACD, Bollinger Bands and more on your actual chart 
- ✓  Plain language explanations — no jargon, no experience required

## Frequently Asked Questions


### What does RSI 70 mean in crypto?

RSI 70 means the asset is in overbought territory — it has risen quickly over recent periods and momentum may be slowing. It does not automatically mean a price drop is coming, but it signals that the pace of gains has been unusually fast. 


### What does RSI 30 mean in crypto?

RSI 30 means the asset is in oversold territory — it has fallen sharply in recent periods. It does not guarantee a bounce, but it indicates that selling pressure has been extreme and momentum may shift. 


### Should I buy when RSI is 30?

RSI 30 is a context clue, not a buy signal. Many assets stay oversold for extended periods during strong downtrends. RSI should be read alongside other indicators and chart structure. This is educational content and does not constitute financial advice. 


### Should I sell when RSI is 70?

RSI 70 does not mean you should sell. During strong uptrends, RSI can stay above 70 for weeks. The level tells you momentum has been strong — it does not predict a reversal. Always look at the broader trend and other indicators. 


### What is a good RSI level for crypto?

There is no universally good or bad RSI level. Readings between 40 and 60 are considered neutral. Above 70 is overbought territory, below 30 is oversold. The significance depends on the timeframe, the asset, and the broader market context. 


### What is RSI divergence in crypto?

RSI divergence occurs when price and RSI move in opposite directions. Bullish divergence: price makes a lower low but RSI makes a higher low — suggests weakening selling pressure. Bearish divergence: price makes a higher high but RSI makes a lower high — suggests weakening buying pressure.
